title: Parastrobilanthes koordersii
taxonomy: Parastrobilanthes koordersii is classified within the family Acanthaceae. The genus Parastrobilanthes is a group of flowering plants native to Southeast Asia. This species, along with others in the genus, is part of the subfamily Acanthoideae. intro: Parastrobilanthes koordersii, also known as the "Java strobilanthes," is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Parastrobilanthes koordersii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to make teas, capsules, and infusions to harness its health benefits.
